title: The Revolt and Death of Amr b. Said in Damascus
description: According to al-Wagidi, `Abd al-Malik b. Marwan went out to 'Ayn Wardah558 this year and made `Amr b. Said b. al-`As559 his deputy over Damascus. The latter fortified himself there. When word of this reached 'Abd al-Malik, he returned to Damascus and besieged him. Thus said al-Wagidi. It is also said that `Amr b. Said went out

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 21
